name: First-species counterpoint (Fux)
description: Note-against-note (first species) counterpoint, after Fux's Gradus ad Parnassum, expressed in the shipped voice_motion + melody families. SCOPE ASSUMPTIONS (documented, not defects): the lower sounding voice is the harmonic bass, so a vertical perfect fourth (interval_class_to 5) is treated as a dissonance; melodic rules apply per analyzed voice line. What this ruleset DELIBERATELY omits because the v1 DSL cannot yet express it (each is recorded roadmap evidence, see integrations/ + ROADMAP gap D): begin/end on a perfect consonance and the contrary-stepwise final (need phrase/global scope); no voice crossing / counterpoint-above-cantus-firmus (needs cross-field comparison of a_to_midi vs b_to_midi — the DSL tests one field against a literal, never two fields against each other); no more than three consecutive parallel thirds/sixths (needs run-length over the sequence — the gap C pattern layer); a single melodic climax (needs a global max over the line). The LOCAL rules below are complete and exact.
